bullets.text: A senior Islamic State leader, Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described as the 'most active terrorist in the world', was reportedly killed in a joint operation by US and Nigerian forces.
An outbreak of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo has claimed 65 lives, with Uganda also reporting cases of the Bundibugyo strain.
Reports suggest the US is planning to charge former Cuban leader Raúl Castro, potentially next week, in a case possibly linked to the 1996 downing of two planes.
China has quietly halted the export of sulphuric acid, a critical industrial chemical used in various essential processes, amid disruptions from the Iran war.
There is a push to update energy standards for rental properties, including proposals for a standard power plug to charge homes from electric vehicles.
Concerns have been raised within the Liberal party about 'dog whistling' on immigration, with some members believing Pauline Hanson's party is influencing the Liberal agenda.
NATO is set to urge European arms manufacturers to increase investment and production, with alliance chief Mark Rutte planning to meet defense groups next week in Brussels.
A rare strain of Ebola, for which there is no approved vaccine or treatment, has been linked to 87 deaths in the conflict-affected northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo.
Israel has reportedly killed Izz al-Din al-Haddad, the last Hamas chief who oversaw the October 7 attack, through an air strike, intensifying pressure on the current fragile ceasefire.
